name: Atlantis
description: Atlantis, a legendary island civilization, holds a prominent place in Western imagination. First described by Plato in his dialogues *Timaeus* and *Critias*, Atlantis is presented as a powerful maritime empire that existed millennia before classical Greece. According to Plato, it was located beyond the Pillars of Hercules (Strait of Gibraltar) and possessed advanced technology, sophisticated social structures, and immense wealth. Atlantean society was initially virtuous, ruled by philosopher-kings, but gradually succumbed to hubris and corruption. This moral decay ultimately led to the civilization's downfall, as punishment from the gods for their arrogance. Atlantis was said to have been destroyed in a cataclysmic event involving earthquakes and floods, sinking beneath the waves and disappearing without a trace. The tale of Atlantis serves as a cautionary allegory about the dangers of unchecked power, materialism, and moral decline. Its enduring appeal has made it a popular subject in literature, film, and various esoteric traditions, often reimagined as a utopian society or a source of ancient wisdom.
subtitle: A legendary lost island civilization.
location: Beyond the Pillars of Hercules (Atlantic Ocean)
significance: Represents the dangers of hubris and the cyclical nature of civilizations.

keyMyths.title: Plato's Account
keyMyths.description: In Plato's dialogues *Timaeus* and *Critias*, Atlantis is introduced as a mighty empire that waged war against Athens. The Atlanteans, initially virtuous, became corrupted by wealth and power, leading to their defeat by the Athenians and subsequent destruction by the gods through earthquakes and floods. The island sank into the ocean, disappearing completely.

keyMyths.title: The Division of Atlantis
keyMyths.description: According to Plato, Poseidon, the god of the sea, fell in love with a mortal woman named Cleito and created Atlantis as her home. He divided the island into ten portions, each ruled by one of his sons. These ten kings formed a powerful alliance, but their descendants eventually grew arrogant and greedy, leading to the civilization's downfall.

keyMyths.title: Atlantis as a Utopian Society
keyMyths.description: In more modern interpretations, Atlantis is often portrayed as a utopian society possessing advanced technology and spiritual wisdom. These accounts often depict Atlanteans as highly evolved beings with psychic abilities and a deep understanding of the universe. The destruction of Atlantis is attributed to a misuse of their advanced technology or a spiritual decline.
keyMyths.source: Various esoteric and New Age texts

sources.author: Plato
sources.description: Plato's dialogue that initially introduces the story of Atlantis as a historical account.
sources.text: Plato's dialogue that initially introduces the story of Atlantis as a historical account.

sources.author: Plato
sources.description: Plato's unfinished dialogue that provides a more detailed description of Atlantean society and its downfall.

sources.author: Ignatius L. Donnelly
sources.description: A 19th-century book that popularized the idea of Atlantis as a real historical place and the origin of all civilizations.
